points: GST initially boosted ICV more; created mix pressure (HD trucks more margin accretive).
Expect tipper + multi-axle as fastest-growing, plus 'trip trailer' (mining-linked).

Management nonetheless stated gross margin resilience via 'better price realizations, rigorous cost-saving efforts and… product and business mix.'
Pricing actions: Jan price increase ~1% supported Q4 realization.
Q1 FY27 price increase 1%–1.5%, with uncertainty on sustaining through the quarter.
Commodity pressure: Management flagged 'significant increase… predominantly steel' entering Q1; refused to quantify magnitude ('too early to give a range').
Mitigation toolkit: value engineering, e-sourcing, supplier negotiations, turnover discounts; plus broader OPEX scrutiny.
CFO: cross-functional teams reviewing 'each of the operating expense… head-wise individually' to drive reductions.

Operating leverage absence in Q4: CFO attributed to one-time provisioning for performance-related bonuses and commodity turbulence; acknowledged analyst observation that Q4 didn’t show typical leverage.
Other cost risks into FY27: potential inflation in transportation expense (fuel-linked), conversion costs, and FX (rupee weakening) impacting 'other expenditure.'
Industry pricing discipline: CEO expects discipline to strengthen; 'industry… should look at taking multiple price hikes during the year' given input inflation.

key_takeaways: Demand is being underwritten by replacement + GST-driven TCO reset, with management repeatedly emphasizing baseline resilience despite diesel noise and localized disruptions.

Near-term margin risk is real and concentrated in commodities (steel) plus fuel-linked opex; company response is measured price increases (1–1.5%) + intensified internal cost programs, but with explicit limits if commodities spike sharply.
Mix and product cycle are turning more favorable if mining/infra-led tippers and multi-axles accelerate and new high-HP products ramp—management expects visible share benefit from Q2.

EV strategy moved from 'investment' to 'execution' milestone with Switch India profitability and a tangible battery pack manufacturing timeline; cell manufacturing remains optional/phase-3 contingent on demand.
Capital deployment is flexible and need-based, with likely incremental support for financing and E-MaaS subsidiaries, while Switch India reduces funding burden due to profitability.

subsections.label: Financial Highlights
points: Revenue for Q2 was at Rs. 9,588 crores, higher by 9.3% on Y-o-Y basis.
EBITDA was at Q2 record level at Rs. 1,162 crores, higher by 14.2% Y-o-Y.
Q2 PBT was also at record levels at Rs. 1,043 crores.
PAT for Q2 was at Rs. 771 crores.
EBITDA margin for the quarter was at 12.1%, higher by 50 basis points against Q2 of last year.
CAPEX for the quarter was Rs. 417 crores and cumulatively Rs. 658 crores for H1.
Cash position net of debt continues to be positive at the end of Q2 at roughly Rs. 1,000 crores, reflecting a positive swing of roughly Rs. 1,500 crores on Y-o-Y basis.
The Board of Ashok Leyland has recommended an interim dividend of INR 1 per share.
Material cost as a percentage of revenue for Q2 was at 71.2% at the same level as same quarter last year.
Our cash position net of debt continues to be positive at the end of Q2 at roughly Rs. 1,000 crores, reflecting a positive swing of roughly Rs. 1,500 crores on Y-o-Y basis.
We have been able to keep our operating capital very lean and have brought it down by almost 50% on Y-o-Y basis.
ROCE was at about 34% last year and return on equity is about 32.5%.

subsections.label: Segment Performance

The aftermarket revenues for Q2 were higher 11% Y-o-Y.
Revenue from power solutions business was higher by 14% Y-o-Y.
Revenue from defense business was higher by 25% Y-o-Y.
Defense order book and tender wind pipeline remains quite strong.

subsections.label: Product Development
points: Our product development pipeline is stronger than ever.

Our non-diesel portfolio is continuously expanding, with two models of light electric trucks, three models of MHCV electric trucks and several models and variants of electric buses already available commercially.
We have also forayed into other greener technologies such as CNG, LNG and even hydrogen.
Within the diesel range, we are preparing to soon launch a completely new range of heavy-duty trucks with power ratings of 320 and 360 horsepower.
These new trucks are built with next-level of heavy-duty aggregates, delivering unmatched reliability.

These products would be fitted with our most modern 6-cylinder engines, delivering highest peak torque in the respective segments, enabling our customer’s best-in-class turnaround time and therefore more earnings per month.
We are also working on improving the throughput of our R&D resources, which will help us reduce the time to market and respond quickly to the changing market and regulatory requirements.

subsections.label: Capacity Expansion
points: We are continuously augmenting our fully-built bus capacity to cater to the growing demand of fully-built buses.
Our newest and most modern bus plant at Lucknow shall be inaugurated soon.
After complete ramp-up of our AP and Lucknow plants, we shall reach bus body-building capacity of 20,000 numbers-plus per year from that of roughly 12,000 numbers at present.
As far as the capacity is concerned, our current capacity is close to 80,000 units on the light commercial vehicles.
We have already laid out a plan to increase this capacity to 110,000-120,000 units without much of an investment.
Mainly these are process changes and some small investments here or there, but largely leading through process changes and efficiency changes.
No more floor area expansion, no more new buildings, etc.

subsections.label: Geographical Expansion
points: We also continue to expand our domestic network.
We added 27 MHCV points and 26 LCV touchpoints during the quarter, with most of the additions happening in northeast and the central areas.
With these additions, total touchpoints are about to touch the milestone number of 2,000, that is 1,100 for MHCV and 876 for LCV.
Internationally as well, we are expanding our network in all our four home markets with SAARC, Africa, GCC and ASEAN.

subsections.label: Supply Chain Management
points: We have been able to keep our operating capital very lean and have brought it down by almost 50% on Y-o-Y basis.
We are running several projects on better cash management, cost management.
We believe there is still a lot of potential left even in inventory ratios, inventory turnover, receivables, there is a lot more juice that is left.

key_figures.label: Domestic truck revenue share in FY '22

subsections.label: Market Strategy
points: Roughly 50% of the revenue comes from the non-truck businesses.
With buses, about 13%; light commercial vehicles, about 12%; spares, about 10% and exports, about 7%-8%.
The margins from these businesses are higher than the domestic truck margins.
The share of domestic truck business was 60% in FY '22, now it has moved to 51%.
Our break-even volume we have been able to reduce drastically.

Break-even volume when considered in terms of the units we have to sell for MHCV trucks used to be about 6,000-7,000 units a month, now it has dropped to more or less closer to 1,000-1,200 units a month.

subsections.label: Strategic Initiatives
points: Ashok Leyland also took a milestone decision to foray into the battery manufacturing business, giving shape to its long-term strategic plans.

Switch India is progressing well on its target of becoming free cash flow positive by FY '27.

HLF has received the final clearance from RBI to initiate a merger process with NXT Digital, paving the way for its listing.
We have now signed four franchisee partners for AL HARIT, our platform for RVSF or Registered Vehicle Scrappage Facilities.
We have progressed really well in our commitment towards RE100, achieving 84% RE status, with our Tamil Nadu plants operating now close to 98% RE.

points: Material cost as a percentage of revenue for Q2 was at 71.2% at the same level as same quarter last year, despite tariff volatilities and adoption of ACs in MHCV trucks.

Commodity costs are expected to be better in Q3 than Q2, with no major concerns for Q4.

quarters.summary: Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd achieved record revenues in FY26, with significant growth in emerging businesses and strategic acquisitions, while maintaining a strong order book and focusing on capacity expansion.

subsections.label: FY26 headline performance and quality of earnings
points: Record scale: FY26 delivered the highest ever quarterly and annual revenues.
Profitability inflection in Q4 attributed to improving profitability in emerging businesses, particularly lighting and electronics and aerospace.
Margins held despite inflation due to operational excellence initiatives, cost optimization programs, and improving efficiencies across plants.

subsections.label: PAT progression and normalization adjustments
points: Q4 normalized PAT +66% YoY due to operating leverage and mix.
Reported PAT included exceptional provisions for business transformative measures in Central and Western Europe.
FY26 also had new Labor Code impact and accelerated amortization of intangibles.

subsections.label: Balance sheet strengthening
points: Leverage ratio at an all-time low of 0.8x, reinforcing the stated policy of <2.5x and internal aspiration of <1.5x.

subsections.label: Order book, portfolio mix, and strategic roadmap (Vision 2030 / 3CX10 / D.E.M.A.L.)
points: All-time-high book business of USD 96 billion, providing strong visibility for future growth.
EV programs account for ~22% of the order book; current EV revenue is ~11%.
Non-automotive accounts for ~3% and continues to grow steadily.
Diversified across PV, CV, off-highway, rolling stock, 2W, and geographies; emerging markets rising in the mix due to growth and manufacturing ecosystems supporting exports under 'globally local'.

subsections.label: Consumer electronics (within Lighting & Electronics / emerging businesses)
points: FY26 revenues increased ~7.5x YoY; Q4 revenues +46% sequentially after the second facility went live in Q3.
Achieved Q4 production run-rate aligned to annual guidance of 14–16 million units.
The business achieved EBITDA profitability during FY26.
Third facility (GF3) commissioning remains on track for Q3 FY27, adding additional upstream integration capabilities.
Management emphasized electronics ramps faster than auto; orders can move straight 0 to 100 once the season/program starts.
GF1+GF2 are almost practically fully used up; the meaningful kick will come as soon as the third plant is operational.
GF3 described as the group’s largest facility—a size of 33 football fields.

subsections.label: Aerospace (non-automotive adjacency with accelerating scale)
points: FY26 revenues +40% YoY; nearly 10x over the last 3 years.
Order book increased >20% to USD 1.6bn, giving multi-year visibility.
Won orders across metallic parts, subassemblies, and wire harness for business jets and rotary wing aircraft.
Two new India facilities expected to come up in Q1 FY27 to serve the existing USD 1.6bn backlog.

subsections.label: Wiring harness and broader automotive operations
points: Wiring harness division saw ~170 bps QoQ margin expansion despite copper inflation and weaker margins in Motherson Wiring India.
North America recovery optionality expected to support further improvement.

subsections.label: Integrated Assemblies / MSAS

subsections.label: Capital allocation, capex pipeline, and shareholder returns
points: FY26 capex: INR 5,911 crore (~49% of EBITDA), allocated to growth, backward integration, and maintenance.

Facility pipeline: 16 facilities under development globally, 13 scheduled to come on stream in FY27; all are in emerging markets.
Final dividend INR 0.25/share; total FY26 dividend INR 0.60/share; payout ratio 16.4%, with an aspiration to move toward up to 40% by Vision 2030.

subsections.label: Supply chain risk mitigation
points: Globally local manufacturing strategy reduced dependence on long-distance supply chains; Red Sea disruptions had minimal impact.
For customer-nominated components: back-to-back supply arrangements to mitigate escalation.

subsections.label: Inorganic growth pipeline and transaction timelines
points: Atsumitec integration: FY26 was the full first year, progress pleased.
Completed acquisition of another Honda San asset.
Yutaka Giken acquisition on track to close by end of H1 FY27.
Nexans automotive harness business positioned as materially enhancing PV/CV harness capabilities globally and enabling cross-selling.
Closing expected end of June, beginning of July, with consolidation thereafter.

subsections.label: Logistics
points: Partnership with Hellmann framed as a move from 3PL to 4PL and more integrated customer solutions.
Capex update included two new wiring harness facilities and two logistics facilities announced since last update.

subsections.label: Cost headwinds, pass-through mechanics, and operational resilience
points: Copper prices increased 16% sequentially in Q4, ~38% YoY.
Middle East tensions led to crude-linked inflation late in the quarter.
Polymer prices in Germany rose significantly post conflict escalation; freight/container costs also increased.
Raw materials have long-term pass-through arrangements with major customers; timing lag of 1–2 quarters acknowledged.
Energy/gas/freight and some overhead-type costs are a matter of negotiations, but management expressed confidence in recovering most due to strong OEM relationships.

subsections.label: Vision 2030 framing
points: FY26 gross revenue disclosed as USD 22.9bn; FY25 restated to USD 21.2bn using constant FX INR 84.55/USD for comparability.
Target reiterated: USD 108bn gross revenues by 2030; management stressed broader discipline: top line is vanity, bottom line is sanity, cash in the bank is reality.
ROCE moderated to 16.1% (from 17.2%) due to capacity build; aspiration reiterated: 40% ROCE across businesses over time.

subsections.label: Management’s view on industry shifts (EV, Europe, and customer behavior)
points: Management reiterated engine agnostic positioning; does not expect a rapid shift to all-EV, citing multi-powertrain futures driven by market pull and policy.
Despite weak macro/volume growth, management expects FY27 European launches to be content heavy and value increasing.
Restructuring in Europe is a big chunk done, but further resizing/efficiency actions may continue alongside acquisitions.

points: Bosch Limited received board approval to acquire Bosch Chassis Systems India Private Limited, a market leader in safety and braking systems.
The acquisition is a strategic move to consolidate mobility solutions and strengthen Bosch Limited's portfolio and market position.
The transaction is structured as an all-cash deal with a small preferential allotment to maintain shareholder involvement.
The acquisition will create a single unified powerhouse, enhancing scalable solutions for every vehicle segment and platform in the market.

points: Quarter-on-quarter, the Mobility business has shown 18.5% growth in October-December 2025 compared to October-December 2024, driven mainly from the Power Solutions business, which grew by 19.5%.

The 2-Wheeler business grew by 58.3%, mainly due to higher sales of exhaust gas sensors due to ramp-up for OBD-II norms implementation.

The Mobility business has grown by 14.9% in April-December 2025 compared to April-December 2024, driven mainly from the Power Solutions business, which grew by 14.1%.

subsections.label: Localization Efforts
points: Bosch is importing mainly components from the parent and is undergoing a process of localization.

points: Looking ahead to fiscal year 2026, Bosch anticipates a period of steady and robust growth for the Indian automotive industry.

The Commercial Vehicle segment is poised for healthy growth fueled by sustained infrastructure spending and continued expansion of e-commerce logistics.

subsections.label: Strategic Partnerships
points: Bosch's partnerships with major global brands underscore the trust placed in its technology to deliver performance and safety.
Bosch continues to shape the future of Mobility by developing next-generation technology.

points: The global economy is adjusting to a landscape reshaped by geopolitical events and realignments in trade relationships.

quarters.summary: Bharat Forge anticipates strong growth in its India operations, driven by aerospace and defense sectors, with a focus on strategic acquisitions and restructuring efforts in Europe.

subsections.label: Quarterly Performance
points: Stand-alone revenue for the quarter was up by 8.5% quarter-over-quarter at INR 2,260 crores.
The quarter-on-quarter improvement in performance was driven by all-around recovery in exports and strong performance in the domestic automotive segment.
Q4 EBITDA was up at 7.2% sequentially at INR 610 crores, translating in EBITDA margin of 27%.
This includes a one-time retrospective cost by MSEDCL of about INR 11 crores. Without that, the margin would have been closer to 28%.
PBT before exceptional items was about INR 486 crores.

subsections.label: Overseas Business
points: Initiated restructuring of German steel business, CDP Bharat Forge, expected to complete by end of next calendar year.
EU operations registered revenue of INR 3,865 crores and EBITDA of INR 151 crores, resulting in EBITDA margin of 4%.
U.S. operations recorded a revenue of INR 1,534 crores, EBITDA of INR 54 crores translating to EBITDA margin of 3.5%.

subsections.label: Order Wins
points: The company secured new businesses for INR 4,814 crores across all key businesses.
Defense orders amounted to INR 2,816 crores.
JSA, the casting unit, secured INR 292 crores in orders.
K-Drive, a recent acquisition, secured about INR 500 crores in orders.

subsections.label: Aerospace Business
points: The aerospace business is now a meaningful part of industrial exports, accounting for almost 26% of the last quarter's non-Auto exports.
This segment has seen multiple new business wins across jet engine structure and landing systems from global OEMs.
Bharat Forge was recently selected by an aerospace OEM as their first supplier from India for critical components.

subsections.label: Defense Business
points: The defense business is spread beyond artillery and vehicles to include small arms, naval solutions, and a variety of unmanned platforms.
Recent order wins have resulted in an order book of close to INR 11,000 crores.
The next 3-4 years will see stable revenue accretion in these sectors.
New opportunities in Europe for the defense business are anticipated.

subsections.label: E-mobility
points: Bharat Forge has decided to take a write-off on investments where immediate revenue and business ramp-up are not expected.
The global trajectory of electric vehicle adoption has shifted from initial expectations.

subsections.label: Capacity Expansion
points: Ongoing capex programs across forging, casting, and products platform will translate into INR 800 crores to INR 850 crores capex in a 15-18 month period.
The acquisition of Fortuna Engineering, a company based in Nashik, is expected to enhance machining capabilities.

subsections.label: Strategic Acquisitions
points: Acquired a 30% stake in Fortuna Engineering for INR 130 crores.
Fortuna Engineering has a revenue of about INR 380 crores and a net cash position of about INR 20 crores.
The acquisition is expected to open doors to new customers and expand the machining capabilities.

subsections.label: Tariff and Geopolitical Risks
points: The year had challenges related to tariffs and geopolitical uncertainties, including a war in the Middle East.
These factors have impacted markets, imports, transportation, and other business aspects.

subsections.label: Growth Outlook
points: Bharat Forge expects a close to 25% growth in its India business barring any further geopolitical crisis.
The highest growth is anticipated in the aerospace sector, followed by defense and components business.
The restructuring of the German steel business, CDP Bharat Forge, is expected to conclude by the end of CY27.

guidance_ledger.statement: Barring any further geopolitical crisis and their impact on demand, we should see a close to 25% growth in our India business.
We will see a strong double-digit growth higher than the overall growth that we are anticipating as a company, significantly higher.
The next 3-4 years will see stable revenue accretion in these sectors.
We expect to complete the process by end of next calendar year.
The CV sector for Q4 India looks very strong and may continue into the first half of next year.
Aerospace segment also we see a very strong growth next year, also year after next because next year we have some new programs and some new capacities coming on line.
Q2 and Q3 should be similar, and hopefully by Q4, we should see an uptick.
Aerospace for this year should be in excess of INR 350 crores.
American Axles should add Rs. 1,000 crores to the consolidated topline for the year.

key_takeaways: Consolidated top-line growth of 6% Y-on-Y in Q2 FY26, with the highest revenue growth in the last 10 quarters.
India revenue grew by 6% Y-on-Y to INR 47.1 billion, with a strong recovery in export markets.
European revenue grew by 4% Y-on-Y to EUR 177 million, despite a challenging demand environment.
Vredestein achieved highest ever volumes in Q2 FY26, continuing strong traction in India.
Apollo Tyres became the lead title and jersey sponsor of the Indian cricket team, enhancing brand visibility.
Settlement reached for Enschede plant closure, with expected positive long-term impact on European profitability.

quarters.summary: Apollo Tyres anticipates stronger topline growth in both India and Europe, with a focus on the replacement segment in India and cost optimization in Europe.

subsections.label: India Financial Performance
points: India revenue for the quarter was INR 47.3 billion, a growth of 3% over the same quarter last year.
The EBITDA for India stood at INR 6.4 billion, a margin of 13.6%, a substantial improvement compared to the 11.2% in the last quarter.
Volume growth in India was flattish, led by a low single-digit growth in replacement and a mid-single-digit growth in OE volumes, negated by a significant decline in exports.

subsections.label: Europe Financial Performance
points: Revenue for Europe was EUR 146 million, flattish over the same period last year, but significantly lower than the previous quarter due to a weak market and seasonality.
The EBITDA for Europe was EUR 16 million, with an EBITDA margin of 10.8% compared to 13.7% for the same quarter last year.
Margins were impacted due to negative market growth across product segments and adverse operating leverage.

subsections.label: Demand Outlook
points: Anticipate stronger topline growth in both India and Europe, with India growth likely led by the replacement segment.
Expect demand momentum to improve in the second half of the fiscal year, with a rebound in infrastructure and mining segments post-monsoon.

subsections.label: Segment Performance
points: In India, truck OEM volumes grew, but there was a decline in passenger car OEM volumes.
In Europe, performed better than the market in passenger car replacement tyres, but still in negative territory due to declines in other segments like spacemaster and agri tyres.

subsections.label: Product Developments
points: Received approvals for supplies to a prominent German passenger vehicle manufacturer in both India and Europe.
Launched ultra-high performance tyre Apollo Aspire 5 for the premium car and SUV category.
Vredestein brand recorded highest ever volumes in Q1.

subsections.label: Manufacturing Developments
points: Intended decision to close production at Enschede plant in summer 2026, with consultation process ongoing.
Obtained ISCC certification for Hungary facility in May 2025.

subsections.label: Inflationary Pressures
points: Inflationary pressures in Europe, particularly in energy and salary costs, have impacted margins.
Raw materials in Europe were up 3% Y-o-Y, affecting margins.

subsections.label: Guidance
points: Expect raw material costs to be slightly lower in Q2 vis-a-vis current levels, with some uncertainty due to exchange rates.
No change in capex guidance for FY’26.

key_takeaways: Q4 was the best ever quarter across the board with highest ever revenue, EBITDA, PAT, BEV revenue, and BEV revenue share.
Inflation has hit the industry hard, with major commodities like steel, aluminum, and copper moving up, impacting margins.
Electrification is back in focus with BEV sales growing significantly in the EU and India.
Sona Comstar won 4 new driveline orders, including 3 from European OEMs, marking the first EV order win from Europe in almost 4 years.
The company closed the year with nearly 1,270 crores of cash, providing optionality to invest and grow.
India's share in the revenue mix has now crossed 50% for the full year, showing effective geographic diversification.

India has doubled in revenue mix, now accounting for 55% of Q3 revenue.

key_takeaways: This has been our best quarter ever across all financial metrics, and proud to report that we have for the first time crossed 1,200 Crore in quarterly revenue and 300 Crore in quarterly EBITDA.
China's restrictions on the supply of heavy rare earth magnets to India continues. In response, we have shifted to alternative motor designs that do not use heavy rare earth magnets.

Uncertainties around US tariffs persisted, during the quarter. However, on a positive note, the US administration has extended the tariff relief related to USMCA content for 5 years, to medium and heavy duty vehicles as well.
Our current RFQ pipeline is the strongest in the history of the company, and almost 3 times compared to the same time last year.
Our BEV revenue share continues to improve to 33% in the nine-month period. This was 30% in H1.
The share of our India business has increased to 55% in Q3. Even as consolidated margins continue to remain at the 25% mark.
Our net order book stands at ₹ 235 billion and the EV portion remains high at 71% of the order book.

quarters.summary: Sona BLW Precision Forgings Ltd achieved its highest-ever quarterly revenue, EBITDA, and net profit in Q2 FY26, despite challenges such as a decline in BEV revenue and supply chain disruptions. The company is optimistic about future growth opportunities, particularly in the EV and railway segments.

subsections.label: Quarterly Performance
points: Achieved highest-ever quarterly revenue, EBITDA, and net profit.
Revenue grew by 24% year on year.
EBITDA and net profit increased by 13% and 20% respectively.
EBITDA margin lower by 2.3% compared to the same quarter last year.
Profit after tax margin lower by 0.6% due to transmission of lower EBITDA margin.

subsections.label: Half-Year Performance
points: Revenue and PAT up by 10% and 4% respectively.
EBITDA lower by 3% year on year.
ROE and ROCE declined due to equity capital issue in September 2024.
Return ratios expected to improve gradually.

subsections.label: Cash Flow and Capital Allocation
points: Generated ₹ 340 crores as cash from operations in the first half.
Spent about ₹ 198 crores on CAPEX.
Major cash outflows on account of railway business acquisition and last tranche payment of NOVELIC acquisition, aggregating to nearly ₹ 1,700 crore.
Dividend distribution of ₹ 100 crores.
Purchase of land for railway business future expansion - ₹ 110 crores.
Left with close to ₹ 1,000 crores of cash at the end of the first half.

Railway business has orders worth ₹ 13 billion, expected to be executed mainly within the next 12 months.

points: BEV revenue declined by 17% in the last quarter.
Revenue share fell to 32%.
Decline primarily due to one customer and linked to a single model.
BEV revenue in rupee terms decreased by 21% to ₹ 4.75 billion.
BEV revenue share declined from 35% in H1 of last year to 30%.

subsections.label: Product Mix and Growth
points: Growth primarily from trains, tractors, and traction motors.
Railway segment, off-highway segment, and electric two-wheelers are growth areas.
Revenue share from non-automotive sectors increased from 10% to 29% in H1.
Off-highway market is a bright spot, especially in the Indian context.

subsections.label: New Product Developments
points: Developed, tested, and validated a rare-earth-free Ferrite Assisted Synchronous Reluctance Motor for electric three-wheelers and light commercial vehicles.
Collaborated with Neura Robotics of Germany to develop advanced components and technologies for industrializing robots, cobots, and humanoids.
Successfully developed and tested rare-earth-free traction motors based on synchronous reluctance technology.

subsections.label: Suspension Systems
points: Nominated for two more programs to supply suspension systems.
One nomination from an existing customer, an Asian EV OEM, and the second from a European OEM of luxury performance cars.
Order value of about ₹ 8.2 billion in the lifetime, expected to start production in Q2 of FY 27.

subsections.label: Mexico Plant
points: Secured first program for the new driveline plant in Mexico to supply differential assemblies to a recreational vehicle OEM in the US.
Program added ₹ 2.6 billion to the order book with production expected to start in the second quarter of FY 28.
Plant has potential to expand and win business from North America and Mexico.

subsections.label: Geographical Revenue Distribution
points: India is the largest end market in H1, accounting for 45% of revenue.
North America is the second largest market, contributing 30%.
Achieved higher revenue from eastern markets than from the West for the first time since going public.

subsections.label: Strategic Collaborations
points: Collaborated with Neura Robotics of Germany to develop advanced components and technologies for industrializing robots, cobots, and humanoids.

subsections.label: Joint Venture with JNT in China
points: Proposed joint venture with JNT in China put in abeyance due to geopolitical factors and prioritization of other capital allocation decisions.
Engagement with JNT remains strong, with potential for future collaboration.

China stopped supplying heavy rare-earth magnets, impacting production of EV traction motors.
Uncertainties regarding USA tariffs persist, but recent US decision to extend tariff relief on vehicles assembled in the US should benefit US customers.
Nexperia chips issue and Novelis plant fire are potential risks being monitored.
